pi_items.source: Found in export_result.sh and verifier.py as the expected target configuration file
Found in verifier.py scoring logic for optimal altitude
Found in verifier.py scoring logic as maximum allowed acceleration
Found in verifier.py scoring logic as minimum rail departure velocity
Found in verifier.py scoring logic as maximum launch rod length limit
Found in verifier.py score evaluation logic (passed = score >= pass_threshold)

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a non-synthetic open-source 38mm 'Janus' model rocket design fetched from the 3dp-rocket GitHub repository. A python script in setup_task.sh modifies the downloaded rocket file by stripping all previous simulations and motor configurations to create the starting rocket file at /home/ga/Documents/rockets/janus_payload.ork. The verifier script actively checks the user's updated .ork file for compliance with explicit kinematic thresholds: an altitude >= 1000m, an acceleration <= 100 m/s², a rail velocity >= 15 m/s, and a launch rod length <= 3.0m, scoring out of 100 with a strict pass_threshold of 70. Both the plot and memo files are strictly evaluated for their presence and creation time to prevent gaming.
